articles.headline: Creatine Is No Longer Just a Gym Supplement
articles.body: A new review published May 4, 2026 takes a deep look at creatine beyond bench press numbers. The findings confirm it helps muscles regenerate ATP during intense effort — but scientists are increasingly excited about what it does for the brain. Studies suggest it may support memory, mood, and processing speed, especially in people with naturally lower creatine levels, like older adults. Researchers are now exploring its potential role in depression, Parkinson's, and menopause-related muscle loss. The headline: this one's not just for gym bros anymore.
articles.takeaway: 5g of creatine monohydrate daily is low-risk, well-researched, and increasingly supported for brain health — not just performance.

product.name: Thorne Creatine Monohydrate
product.blurb: The most researched supplement in history. Works for strength, brain health, and endurance.

articles.topic: CAG-170 gut bacteria health marker global study
articles.headline: Scientists Just Found a Hidden Gut Bacteria That Tracks With Good Health
articles.body: A University of Cambridge team analyzed over 11,000 gut samples from 39 countries and discovered a previously overlooked group of gut bacteria called CAG-170. People with chronic diseases — including inflammatory bowel disease, obesity, and chronic fatigue syndrome — had consistently lower levels of it. The bacteria appear stable over time, support the wider gut ecosystem, and may produce vitamin B12. The lead researcher noted that most probiotic products haven't caught up with this kind of discovery yet.
articles.takeaway: A varied, fiber-rich diet is still your best tool for supporting a balanced microbiome while the science on CAG-170 develops.

articles.topic: sleep quality versus quantity cognitive performance older adults
articles.headline: How Well You Sleep Matters More Than How Long
articles.body: Penn State researchers tracked sleep and cognitive performance in older adults and found a clear pattern: the quality of a night's sleep predicted how quickly people processed information the next day — not the length. The key culprit was how much time people spent awake after falling asleep. More wake time meant slower processing and worse visual working memory scores. The lead author was direct: out of all the sleep factors they measured, quality was the only one that made a day-to-day difference in cognitive performance.
articles.takeaway: If you're waking up multiple times at night, that's the problem worth solving — even if your total hours look fine on paper.

product.name: LectroFan White Noise Machine
product.blurb: Blocks ambient noise so your sleep is actually restorative.

articles.topic: dehydration sustained attention middle-aged adults daily activities
articles.headline: You Might Be Too Dehydrated to Focus — Without Even Knowing It
articles.body: Penn State researchers tracked 78 adults between ages 47 and 70 over three months, measuring actual hydration levels via blood markers. At each check-in, up to 39% of the group was dehydrated — from nothing more than normal daily activities. Those participants performed significantly worse on a sustained attention task lasting 14 minutes. Working memory and other cognitive tests were unaffected. The finding matters because most of our important work tasks — reading, writing, analyzing — require exactly that kind of extended focus.
articles.takeaway: Don't wait until you're thirsty. Keep a water bottle at your desk and sip consistently through the day, especially before long work blocks.

product.name: Skratch Labs Hydration Mix
product.blurb: Clean hydration without artificial junk. What Chris uses on long training days.

quote.author: Mark Twain
quote.context: American author and humorist, known for his sharp wit and timeless observations on human nature.

workout.title: Dead Hang and Row Grinder
workout.warmup: Start with 2 minutes of arm circles, shoulder rolls, and cat-cow stretches on the floor. Then do 2 sets of 5 slow, controlled dead hangs lasting 10 seconds each to open up the shoulders and grip.

exercises.name: Dead Hang
exercises.notes: Hang from a bar with a full grip, shoulders pulled slightly down away from ears. Use a band for support if needed. Builds grip and decompresses the spine.

exercises.name: Scapular Pull-Up
exercises.notes: From a dead hang, retract and depress your shoulder blades without bending your elbows. Pause at the top. This primes the upper back before full pull-up work.

exercises.name: Resistance Band Bent-Over Row
exercises.notes: Stand on band, hinge at hips to roughly 45 degrees, and pull elbows back behind your body. Keep core braced. Squeeze hard at the top for a 1-second hold.

exercises.name: Chin-Up or Band-Assisted Chin-Up
exercises.notes: Palms face you. Pull chest toward bar, not chin over bar. Control the descent for 3 counts. Use a looped band under your knee if needed.

exercises.name: Plank with Shoulder Tap
exercises.notes: Hold a strong plank, feet hip-width apart to reduce rotation. Tap opposite shoulder while keeping hips level. Slower is harder — make it count.

workout.cooldown: Finish with a doorway or band chest-opener stretch for 60 seconds, then a 90-second lat stretch — grab a stable post or doorframe and sit back into your hip to lengthen the side body.

product.name: Gymnastic Rings - Double Circle Wood
product.blurb: Chris trains on rings 2x per week. The most effective upper body tool that fits in a bag.

meal.title: Lemon Herb Salmon Rice Bowl
ingredients: 1 cup cooked brown rice or microwaveable rice pouch
1 cup baby spinach or mixed greens
Salt, pepper, and dried dill to taste
Optional: 1 tbsp low-sodium soy sauce or tamari drizzle
meal.instructions: Season salmon with garlic powder, dill, salt, and pepper. Pan-sear in olive oil over medium-high heat for 3-4 minutes per side until cooked through. While salmon cooks, heat your rice and lay it in a bowl with greens, cucumber, and avocado. Flake salmon over the top, squeeze lemon juice over everything, and finish with a drizzle of tamari if using.
meal.tip: Buy a bag of microwave rice pouches and keep salmon fillets in the freezer — this bowl comes together in under 15 minutes on even the most slammed weekdays.

product.name: Greater Goods Digital Food Scale
product.blurb: Measure your food for two weeks and you will understand your body better than any diet book.
